description:
## Goal

Add a non-interactive REPL driver: `fz repl --script <path>` reads the file, feeds it line-by-line through the same `try_eval` loop the interactive REPL uses, captures only program-side `print()` output to stdout, and after EOF invokes `main/0` if defined.

## Changes

### src/repl.rs
- New `pub fn run_script(path: &Path) -> io::Result<()>`. Mirrors `run()` but:
  - Input is the file's lines instead of stdin.
  - No banner. No `fz> ` / `... ` prompts.
  - On `Outcome::Incomplete`, continue buffering (same as interactive).
  - On `Outcome::Err`, print to stderr and exit nonzero.
  - After EOF, look up `main/0` in `interp.globals`; if present, evaluate `main()` via the same path the interactive REPL uses for expressions.
- Audit `try_eval`: if it currently prints anything besides program-driven `print()` output (e.g., echoing values of bound expressions, fn-def confirmation), gate that behind an `interactive: bool` flag so `run_script` suppresses it. Goal: only `print()` calls reach stdout.

### src/main.rs
- `fz repl --script <path>` dispatches to `repl::run_script`.
- `fz repl` with no args keeps interactive behavior.

## Acceptance

- `fz repl --script fixtures/add1/input.fz` prints `42\n` to stdout and nothing else. Exits 0.
- A fixture with a parse error: `fz repl --script` exits nonzero, error on stderr, no partial program output on stdout.
- The five existing `repl.rs` interactive tests still pass.
- New test exercising `run_script` against an inline fixture string.
title: REPL .1: fz repl --script <path> non-interactive entry point
